title: sesbania coccinea var. atollensis
intro: Sesbania coccinea var. atollensis, also known as the Red Stork's Bill, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the legume family and is native to the atolls of the Pacific Ocean. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Sesbania coccinea var. atollensis include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. The plant is typically prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment, and can also be used to make a poultice or salve for topical application.
